```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))
## Parametros
lambda <- sample(c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5), 1)
n      <- sample(c(25, 50, 100), 1)

## Gerar sum_x de forma reprodutivel
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))
noise  <- runif(1, -2, 2)
sum_x  <- round(n / lambda + noise, 2)

## EMV de lambda: lambda_hat = n / sum_x
correct <- round(n / sum_x, 4)

## Alternativas erradas
w1 <- round(sum_x / n, 4)           # inverso do correto
w2 <- round(n / (sum_x + 1), 4)     # denominador errado
w3 <- round((n - 1) / sum_x, 4)     # n-1 no lugar de n
w4 <- round(n^2 / sum_x, 4)         # n^2 no numerador

## Garantir unicidade
vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
if (length(unique(round(vals, 5))) < 5) w4 <- w4 + 0.001

all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========
Para $X_i \overset{\text{iid}}{\sim} \text{Exp}(\lambda)$ com $f(x;\lambda) = \lambda\,e^{-\lambda x}$, a forma exponencial identifica $T(x) = x$ e a estatistica suficiente minimal e $T = \sum_{i=1}^n X_i$.

Com $n = `r n`$ observacoes e $\displaystyle\sum_{i=1}^n X_i = `r sum_x`$, o EMV de $\lambda$ (que e funcao da estatistica suficiente $T$) e $\hat{\lambda} = n/\sum X_i$. Qual e o valor do EMV?

Answerlist
----------
* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========
A log-verossimilhanca e:
$$\ell(\lambda) = n\log\lambda - \lambda\sum_{i=1}^n X_i.$$

Maximizando: $\dfrac{d\ell}{d\lambda} = \dfrac{n}{\lambda} - \sum X_i = 0 \implies \hat{\lambda} = \dfrac{n}{\sum X_i}.$

Para $n = `r n`$ e $\sum X_i = `r sum_x`$:
$$\hat{\lambda} = \frac{`r n`}{`r sum_x`} = `r correct`.$$

Note que $\hat{\lambda} = 1/\bar{X}$, que e a funcao da estatistica suficiente $T = \sum X_i$ (ou equivalentemente $\bar{X}$).

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap4S3Q10_Exp_forma_canonica_EMV
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
